exercises.title: Greeting with Variables
exercises.description: In Pyodide, input() is not available. Instead, use pre-set variables. Given name = 'Alice' and city = 'Tokyo', print a greeting: 'Hello, Alice!' and then 'You are from Tokyo.'
exercises.starterCode:
# Variables are pre-set (simulating user input)
name = "Alice"
city = "Tokyo"

# TODO: Print 'Hello, Alice!' using an f-string
# TODO: Print 'You are from Tokyo.' using an f-string
exercises.expectedOutput:
Hello, Alice!
You are from Tokyo.
hints: The variable name goes inside curly braces {} within the f-string.

exercises.title: Profile Card with f-strings
exercises.description: Use pre-set variables to build a formatted profile. Given the variables below, print a profile card showing all four fields with labels.
exercises.starterCode:
# Pre-set variables (simulating user input)
full_name = "Carlos Rivera"
age = 22
hobby = "painting"
favorite_food = "tacos"

# TODO: Print 'Name: Carlos Rivera'
# TODO: Print 'Age: 22'
# TODO: Print 'Hobby: painting'
# TODO: Print 'Favorite food: tacos'
exercises.expectedOutput:
Name: Carlos Rivera
Age: 22
Hobby: painting
Favorite food: tacos
hints: Use f-strings for each line: print(f"Name: {full_name}")
The age variable is a number — f-strings handle that automatically.

exercises.title: Story Generator
exercises.description: Use the given variables to generate a short story. Combine them into a multi-line story using f-strings.
exercises.starterCode:
# Pre-set variables
hero = "Maya"
superpower = "flying"
villain = "Dr. Doom"
location = "New York"

# TODO: Print a 3-line story using the variables above:
# Line 1: 'Maya discovered the power of flying.'
# Line 2: 'When Dr. Doom threatened New York, Maya knew what to do.'
# Line 3: 'Using flying, Maya saved the day!'
exercises.expectedOutput:
Maya discovered the power of flying.
When Dr. Doom threatened New York, Maya knew what to do.
Using flying, Maya saved the day!
hints: Use f-strings to insert each variable: f"{hero} discovered..."
Write one print() call per line of the story.

warmup.title: Quick Recall: f-strings
warmup.description: f-strings insert variable values into text. What does this print?
warmup.starterCode:
# Predict the output before running
name = "Priya"
age = 25
print(f"My name is {name} and I am {age} years old.")
print(f"In 10 years I will be {age + 10}.")
warmup.expectedOutput:
My name is Priya and I am 25 years old.
In 10 years I will be 35.
